name: Pizza Rustica
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds swiss chard, washed well, stems removed and sliced thin
2 pounds swiss chard leaves, chopped separately
15 container whole milk ricotta cheese (up to 16 ounces)
4 large red bell peppers, roasted, peeled, and chopped
2 garlic cloves, minced and mashed into a paste with 1 teaspoon coarse salt
6 ounces provolone cheese, cut into 1/4 inch dice
Egg wash made by beating 1 large egg with 2 tablespoons milk
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy saucepan, cook chard stems in oil over moderately low heat, stirring occasionally, until crisp tender (about 3 minutes).
2. Add chopped swiss chard leaves and cook, covered, over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, until stems are tender (about 8 minutes). Drain well in a colander and squeeze out as much moisture as possible.
3. In a large bowl, combine beaten eggs and ricotta cheese.
4. In another bowl, mix roasted peppers and garlic paste.
5. Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
6. On a lightly floured surface, roll out two-thirds of the pastry dough into a round about 18 inches in diameter, 1/8-inch thick. Fit dough into a 9-inch springform pan and trim edge, leaving a 2-inch overhang.
7. In the shell, layer evenly half of the chard mixture, half of the ricotta mixture, all roasted pepper mixture, half of the Parmesan, all proscuitto, all provolone, remaining ricotta mixture, remaining chard, and remaining Parmesan.
8. On a lightly floured surface, roll out remaining dough into a round about 11 inches in diameter. Brush edge of shell with some egg wash and fit dough on top of filling. Trim the top crust even with the bottom crust and crimp edges decoratively.
9. Cut four 3-inch long vents in the top crust and brush with some remaining egg wash.
10. Bake in the middle of the oven for 1 1/2 hours, or until the top crust is deep golden. Cool completely on a rack. Pizza Rustica may be made 2 days ahead and chilled in pan, covered. Serve warm or at room temperature, cut into wedges.
Yield: 6 servings
